text: Multisensor systems and the deluge of big data have exposed the limitations of classical flat-view matrix based methods and models. Higher-order tensors or multiway arrays provide a new paradigm and new models that are inherently polynomial. In contrast to matrix methods, tensor models have a unique solution under very mild and therefore more than likely natural conditions. Exploiting the mathematical power of multilinear algebra, tensor decompositions provide possibilities to impose constraints that are more in line with the inherent properties of the data. In particular, these models are capable of capturing more complex hidden patterns in the data than any of their matrix based counterparts.
explanation: The text is technical and well-structured, but includes some generic, slightly awkward phrases (e.g., 'inherently polynomial', 'unique solution under very mild conditions') that are commonly produced by AI writers, suggesting a moderate likelihood of AI generation.
